summary: The app writes HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\UseCompactMode = 1, the 25H2 raw registry dump lists the same value name under both the machine and current-user Explorer\Advanced branches, and Procmon captures on 2026-03-14 confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries this exact value on shell restart with both Data:1 and Data:0 when the value is toggled. A Ghidra headless pass on 2026-03-26 against ExplorerFrame.dll also decompiled the code path that calls RegGetValueW for UseCompactMode from both HKCU and HKLM Explorer\Advanced. A rerun on 2026-03-27 against baseline-20260327-regprobe-visible-shell-stable completed with status ok, stopped WPR cleanly, and kept Explorer, sihost, ShellHost, and ctfmon healthy through both candidate and restore restarts. That validates UseCompactMode as a live runtime Explorer preference on this build, resolves the old direction mismatch, and closes the earlier incident-review concern that only affected the older shell-stable baseline.

setting.name: Enable Explorer Compact View
setting.casual_explanation: Explorer compact view reduces spacing between items so more files fit on screen. Some users like the denser layout, while others prefer the roomier default view.
setting.why_people_change_it: People enable it when they want a denser file list and more visible items in Explorer.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Compact view can show more items at once, but it also reduces spacing and can feel more crowded. This record is validated as a runtime-observed Explorer preference rather than as a Microsoft-documented policy contract.
setting.professional_notes: The app writes UseCompactMode = 1 under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced. The 25H2 raw registry dump lists UseCompactMode under both the machine Explorer\Advanced branch and the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch, which corroborates that the value family is still present on current builds. On 2026-03-14, Procmon captures of Explorer restart showed Explorer.EXE querying UseCompactMode with Data:1 and Data:0 in separate reversible captures. On 2026-03-26, a Ghidra headless pass against ExplorerFrame.dll decompiled the RegGetValueW path for UseCompactMode and showed fallback reads from HKCU first and then HKLM under Explorer\Advanced. On 2026-03-27, the runtime lane was rerun against baseline-20260327-regprobe-visible-shell-stable and completed with status ok, WPR stop succeeded, both candidate and restore restarts completed, and explorer, sihost, ShellHost, and ctfmon remained healthy without snapshot recovery. That proves the value is consumed by Explorer shell code on this build and that the earlier runtime incidents were baseline-specific rather than value-specific. No primary Microsoft Learn or ADMX source for UseCompactMode was captured in this pass, so the record is validated as a runtime-observed preference. The old app id explorer.disable-compact-mode was directionally wrong and is now retained only as a legacy id.

allowed_values.label: Compact view disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Procmon confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries UseCompactMode = 0 on shell restart. This is the observed non-compact state on this build.

allowed_values.label: Compact view enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Procmon confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries UseCompactMode = 1 on shell restart. This is the observed compact-view state used by the app.

targets.notes: Validated as a runtime-observed Explorer preference. The absent-value default was not captured in this pass.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes UseCompactMode = 1, and Procmon confirmed that Explorer.EXE reads the same value as a live preference on shell restart. The tweak id now matches the observed direction of the value.

windows_defaults.label: Default state not captured
windows_defaults.applies_to: Explorer user profiles where UseCompactMode is absent

states.rationale: This pass validated the 0 and 1 states through Procmon, but it did not capture the absent-value default.

windows_defaults.notes: Use this record as a validated runtime preference map for 0 and 1, not as a complete Microsoft-documented default model.

recommended_profiles.label: Enable Explorer compact view
intended_for: Users who want denser file lists
avoid_for: Users who prefer more spacing between Explorer items

states.rationale: Set the runtime-observed preference to the compact-view state that Explorer reads on this build.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Explorer to fit more items on screen.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the roomier default spacing.
tradeoffs: The interface can feel more crowded.

evidence.title: 25H2 raw registry corroboration for UseCompactMode
evidence.summary: The 25H2 raw registry dump lists UseCompactMode under both the machine Explorer\Advanced branch and the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch, corroborating that the value family is still present on current builds.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Explorer UseCompactMode runtime reads
evidence.summary: Procmon captured Explorer.EXE querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\UseCompactMode with Data:1 and Data:0 in separate reversible captures after Explorer restart. That proves Explorer consumes this registry value on this build.

evidence.title: Our Ghidra decompilation - ExplorerFrame UseCompactMode handlers
evidence.summary: A headless Ghidra pass on ExplorerFrame.dll decompiled the code paths that call RegGetValueW for UseCompactMode under SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced, including HKCU-first and HKLM fallback reads. That gives direct code-side proof that the Explorer shell consumes this flag.

evidence.title: Incident reviewed - Explorer compact mode runtime rerun on visible-shell baseline
evidence.summary: Incident reviewed on 2026-03-27. The runtime lane was rerun on baseline-20260327-regprobe-visible-shell-stable and completed with status ok. WPR stopped cleanly, both candidate and restore Explorer restarts completed, and explorer, sihost, ShellHost, and ctfmon remained healthy without recovery or snapshot revert. This closes the prior incident-review concern from the older baseline.

decision.why: UseCompactMode is validated as a live Explorer runtime preference because Explorer.EXE queried both 1 and 0 on shell restart in reversible Procmon captures on this build, ExplorerFrame.dll decompiled to direct RegGetValueW reads for the same value under Explorer\Advanced, the app writes the same enabled state, and the 25H2 raw registry dump corroborates that the value family is still present on current builds.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: explorer_batch_applied_20260314.pml: Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\UseCompactMode Data:1. explorer_compact_zero_20260314.pml: Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\UseCompactMode Data:0.
validation_proof.notes: The value was toggled from 1 to 0 and the original absent state was then restored. Both observed states were queried by Explorer.EXE on restart.
